mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns are negative in >50% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Predictive accuracy drops to random chance (Rank IC < 0.01) when inflation volatility exceeds 1.0% annually, or the signal reverses direction during high inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports household income and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive payroll growth or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5 percent annually triggers expectations of monetary tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation threshold crossings or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and accommodative federal funds rates creates a favorable macroeconomic backdrop that amplifies equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses explanatory power (F-stat < 2.0) in multivariate regression or forward returns are negative in >50% of co-occurring expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales below 0.2 percent signals weakening consumer demand that immediately pressures corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances or the signal fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward testing.
